question: Can expert-Muon's optimizer-state memory saving materialize into an MBS8 8xMI350 run if activation checkpointing is scoped away from collective-bearing MoE?

root_cause_read.why_muon_used_more_memory_before: Muon halves persistent expert optimizer state versus AdamW, but native/per-expert Muon introduced large transient rank-2 view/update/orthogonalization state and slow Newton-Schulz work. Batched chunked Muon fixed the isolated optimizer transient-memory regression, but no-AC MBS8 remained activation/backward-memory limited.
root_cause_read.why_whole_block_selective_ac_failed: Whole-transformer selective activation checkpointing recomputed the standard-EP hot-helper MoE path, including route counts, scatter, and MORI/SDMA hot-weight collectives. That exposed checkpoint placeholder dtype issues and unsafe collective replay.
root_cause_read.fix: Keep MoE outside checkpoint recompute by adding an attention-only selective activation-checkpoint scope. Replace torch.histc route counts with integer torch.bincount so selective recompute does not hit histogram_cpu on Byte placeholders.

route_counts: sources/wip/torchtitan/torchtitan/models/common/moe.py replaces hot-split/router torch.histc counts with _count_route_ids via torch.bincount.
sources/wip/torchtitan/torchtitan/models/common/token_dispatcher.py replaces standard-EP dispatch torch.histc counts with _count_route_ids via torch.bincount.
code_changes.launcher: launchers/torchtitan_direct_deepseek_v4_4x8xmi350_canary.sh forwards CANARY_ACTIVATION_CHECKPOINT_SCOPE into the remote and Docker environments.

failed_gates_before_fix.failure: NotImplementedError: histogram_cpu not implemented for Byte
failed_gates_before_fix.read: Whole-block selective AC recomputed through MoE hot-split route-counting code under checkpoint placeholder state.

failed_gates_before_fix.failure: RuntimeError: Autograd not support dtype: Byte
failed_gates_before_fix.read: The route-count fix moved the failure forward, but whole-block checkpointing still replayed the MoE hot-helper scatter path in an invalid recompute context.

failed_gates_before_fix.failure: HSA/GPU hang in MORI SDMA hot-weight all-reduce during checkpointed forward recompute
failed_gates_before_fix.read: Switching scatter backend did not solve the root issue: whole-block selective AC re-entered collective-bearing MoE.

decision.read: This fixes the root-cause path enough to make MBS8 + expert-Muon run six steps and slightly exceed retained AdamW throughput. It is not a clean retained baseline because it uses NS=1 Muon quality-risk semantics and peaks at 243.22 GiB, about 49.42 GiB above the retained MBS4 AdamW baseline.
decision.next_action: Keep attention-only selective AC as the valid memory-scope fix. For promotion, either validate Muon quality at NS=1 and reduce the 243 GiB peak, or use the scoped AC mechanism with AdamW/other memory reducers while continuing MoE/EP and sparse-MLA kernel work.
